lines.content: Hey, this is Jack with Einstein Moving Company. How can I help you out today?
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Hey, Jack, I was just talking to one of your associates but then I don't, I don't know I think the call dropped.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: I'm just wondering if I get a more I have a current CLO, I don't think it's completely accurate. So I was wondering if I can get it a quoted a little bit more accurately and if you guys do like in person estimates.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Do we don't do any in person estimates? But we can definitely take a look at it and I can let you know what I'm thinking. Did you, did you submit information on our website? And then they and then we followed up or did you just call in for?
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Yeah, you can pull it up over my email I'm pretty sure.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Okay, mad thing it looks like which, so are you moving out of town moving to Austin? Okay, so the biggest thing that definitely inflates that quote without speaking to someone is marked with packing. Were you needing us to come in and box everything up for you or were you going to have that boxed up?
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Yeah, so that's the thing. I don't, I was going to be somewhere in between and I think I'm gonna be able to box some stuff up but like, I don't wanna say, I can box everything up, you know what I mean?
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Yeah, so the way that, the way that we work is, so it's just hourly rate is, is basically, it. And we do moving and packing we just you know, we don't bring boxes or packing materials. Unless we're scheduled for packing included in our moving service. We will wrap everything and blankets and shrink wrap, we will take apart bed frames, put them back together for you pretty much anything. And everything is included besides packing of the boxes. So if you wanted us to do some packing, we would just need more time and then the cost of the cost of those boxes. But those are the only, yeah, that's the only added thing. So, that's why the quote was so high was they had four hours of full packing. Cause if we were to, if we were to pack the entire one bedroom, it is mostly just like a one bedroom.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Yeah, so here's the other problem. So it's a three bedroom townhome except I'm only moving one bedroom of it, I'm just one of the roommates but I'm on the second floor and then I'm moving like some stuff in the living room and some kitchen and stuff like, if I'm being completely honest, the three biggest items I see is like a pretty big desk, a bed mattress a couch and like four bar stools and like four car tires and the rest is just like kitchen items and close.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Which I can realistically pack myself?
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Yeah, I was gonna say the main, the main most time consuming part with packing would just be packing of like the kitchen and stuff and how much if we were to do like that packing do, is there a lot of glasses and stuff to pack or is it pretty minimal in the kitchen?
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: No, I have like one per amount for one person. I am like $22. I don't own a lot of stuff, you know what I mean, it's that.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Cool the main thing is, so typically if we're especially with going to Austin, we can do a one bedroom, we can you know, do there and back and, and keep it all within one day. When we add a little bit of packing that starts to make that to be able to go, you know, flow pack drive unload drive back starts, it become quite a lot in one day. If we were to do packing we would probably need to split this into two days just to be safe. That being said we can always, I can still have to bring a few boxes just in case you don't finish things. But let me, I, based on what you tell me, I think we should be. We can probably get this done in one day even with a little bit of pack it seems like it's gonna be pretty easy. And so let's see that would be.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Let's just say it took us.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Three ish hours to get everything loaded, three hours to drive. Some, some time to unload in a single day. Let's just call that basically about a if we were to, so a typical one bedroom, apartment takes about three to four hours to complete locally. So, then if we add in that extra three hour drive that brings you about seven, if they were to do some packing, brings me to kind of about a full eight, maybe eight and a half with some traffic, if we were to say, do eight and a half hours plus that travel fee.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Then you'd be looking at a total of $2,667 basically with us it's just that $96 an hour per mover, so the $192 an hour from the time that we start moving until the time that we finish unloading everything in Austin, if we're able to finish if we finished a lot faster than that's how much you pay for everything is prorated fifteen minutes out of time. But basically estimating about four to five hours of labor plus that three hour drive. And then if you, anything that you get packed up or organized would help bring down that time. But do you have any questions about that the pricing or, or that quote?
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: So like, let's say Prestage everything other than like my desk and bed and like, basically get everything else prepacked what am I, like, I can make that deal, like let's say you think seven hours if possible.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: I think it's possible we generally try to be safe with our quotes. I don't want to tell you, yeah, we can do it in six or seven hours, and have that not be the case. But I, it's definitely realistic. I've definitely, you know, it does happen before when we show up and it's like, oh, this is, if we're able to get out of there in, in two hours or less then you're going to be probably pretty set for that for, for seven hours or less. So, just mostly depends on us getting out of that first location.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: So do you like worst move ever? It's not going past 222300.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: If we, if this was, again, if this was a lot, this is like a full, a lot of one bedroom apartment, then I would say probably closer to that 2592 number which I for that eight and a half hours would probably be for really full apartment so I do think you probably, you know safer underneath that. But yeah, I would kind of keep 8:00 to eight and a half hours it's kind of like a top end number for you. And, and just.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: And what's that eight and a half hour quote?
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Just that, that'd be eight and a half hours, plus the one time $960 travel fee would come out to $2,592. If we hypothetically, if we did it in seven hours you'd be looking at a total of $2,304. So, again it's just based on the time that we spent.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Okay, so your travel fee is really the premium here as well. I compared to other quotes to them.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: The main, yeah, the big thing with moving to Austin is that $960 travel fee and that covers our return journey. So that once the move once we finish unloading that's when your time stops and then that $960 covers all of our drive back and.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: So, is the hourly rate including the travel or no?
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: So, the hourly rate includes when everything is on our truck from when we're driving from north originals to Austin that time is billed at the hourly rate cause it we're moving it on our truck. And then the travel fee covers our initial drive out there and then our return from Austin at the end of the.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Oh, so, so I'm double charging. That's okay so still about, so I would, like, let's worst comes to worse, like, $2,800 kind of thing.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Okay, and this covers like insurance and everything like that, like what should I expect any hidden fees or anything like that?
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Nope. That's the big thing with us is that our rate can be you know, expensive! And a little bit of a sticker shock but the reason why is that we include almost every single thing into that hourly rate. The only time we add those extra fees is a travel fee when we're going, you know, out of, out of the city. So, included in that rate is our no worries damage coverage. Which includes up to 2000 dollars of value based coverage. So, it's not weight based, like most companies, most companies it's sixty cents per pound. And then if you want additional coverage, you have to pay for it and then you have deductibles, and it's still a weight based thing with us, just included, it's value based. So, if there's like a TV, obviously, then you would get paid out based on the value of that item not just the weight of it. So, that's a huge, a huge plus that's included in our rate. Also.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: All of our movers are, you know, super highly trained you're not paying for like contract labors guys that you know, don't speak English or anything like that. You're getting really reputable guys. And then on time guarantees we're gonna wrap everything, we're not gonna damage anything all that sort of stuff. No stair fees, no, no hidden gas mileage fee, no, three percent credit card surcharge fee, literally no taxes. It is just the $192 an hour from the time that we start until the time that we finish it's prorated fifteen minutes out of time. So you just pay every quarter, it's billed every quarter hour and then the one time $960 for us to drive back and that'.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Okay. And then is, is this a two person Q3 person? Like I don't really like everyone else has been quoting me two person queue, I think this was quoted as three.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: It, no. This is, this is our two person Crew2 persons. The typical crew size that we sent.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Okay. Yeah, fair enough. I mean, that kinda, yeah, it's just your guys travel fee is a look like almost double all the other quotes. But I mean, you have all the guarantees and stuff. So, let me, I want to think about this a little bit longer but can you send me over the updated quote.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Yes, sir and then yeah, just double-check on the extra fees, they might be hitting your less on, on an outright travel fee, but then maybe hitting on the back end with a bunch of percentage and mileage and stuff like, stuff like that. So, just double-check the contracts that they're asking you to send, I don't speak against other companies but I've seen just in my moving experience, different people with different companies, get all these weird hidden stuff in there, and so that's the big thing with us is that it's just not and if it is as easy as you say and everything's prepped for us and we're able to finish a lot faster than that's how you much paid for. Yeah, I'll send you an updated quote and then if you do wanna move forward all you gotta do is just respond to that email and say let's do it and then we can hopefully get you set up and scheduled you know, weekends are getting pretty busy here in the summer. So the sooner you let us know the better chance it'll still be available.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Okay. Yeah, go ahead and send that over I mean I'm pretty much lean to you all like I'll be transparent just like call chunks gave me like a 1.7 estimate but the reviews are scary to say the least.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: That is the, that is the risk with other companies and all that stuff and it's, you know, moving as you could get a good crew from them. But you could get, you could get not so much. So there's a little bit of a kind of volatility on those sort of things but I'll send you this new quote and then again just respond to that email or give us a call if you'd like to move forward.
lines.name: Jack Davis

lines.content: Man, I do really appreciate it. Thank you.
lines.name: Rishvvi Pooskur

lines.content: Of course. Alright, have a good one. Bye, bye-bye.
lines.name: Jack Davis
